title: BUG REPORT: podman build may run into storage problems when a step cause lot of changes
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
The problem occurred on a test-platform, that helps to check, if a systemupgrade can 
be performed on the same productive environment. The productive is a Fedora F33-server 
server host and a F33-container os. The container is used to run a root-less accounting
web-application. 
This application runs on the top of a Fedora-Linux, using apache, perl and latex for printing,
and is connected to a postgres-DB running on the server itself. The container and its
services is driven by a systemd, that is running in the container too. You can call him
a fatty container.

While building `podmain build` the container performs a an update on the recent fedora (it must be now  F34)  and then performs lot of updates. After this he trys to load additional packages to run the accounting app.

`
FROM fedora


\# perform updates 
RUN dnf -y update
\# perform install (disabled php-fpm)
RUN dnf -y install  `[[ ${POSTGR_HOST} != '127.0.0.1' &&  ${POSTGR_HOST} != 'localhost' ]] || echo postgresql-server`\
  httpd mod_fcgid  postgresql-contrib\
  perl-Algorithm-CheckDigits perl-Archive-Zip perl-Archive-Extract-zip-Archive-Zip perl-CPAN perl-Class-XSAccessor \
  perl-Clone perl-Config-Std perl-DBD-Pg perl-DBI perl-Daemon-Generic \
  perl-DateTime perl-DateTime-Set perl-Email-Address perl-Email-MIME perl-FCGI \
  perl-File-Copy-Recursive perl-File-Flock perl-File-MimeInfo perl-File-Slurp \
  perl-GD perl-HTML-Restrict perl-JSON perl-List-MoreUtils perl-List-UtilsBy \
  perl-Net-SMTP-SSL perl-Net-SSLGlue perl-PBKDF2-Tiny perl-PDF-API2 \
  perl-Params-Validate perl-Regexp-IPv6 perl-Rose-DB perl-Rose-DB-Object \
  perl-Rose-Object perl-Sort-Naturally perl-String-ShellQuote \
  perl-Template-Toolkit perl-Text-CSV_XS perl-Text-Iconv perl-URI perl-XML-Writer \
  perl-YAML perl-libwww-perl cpanminus \
  aqbanking poppler-utils  \
  php-pecl-apcu php-cli php-pear php-pdo php-mysqlnd php-pgsql php-gd  php-xml php-zipstream \ 
  texlive-scheme-medium texlive-collection-latex texlive-collection-latexextra \
  texlive-collection-latexrecommended texlive-collection-langgerman \
  texlive-collection-langenglish\
  langpacks-de git procps-ng
`
The most recent intall will kill him, when podman tries to commit that step:
`
Complete!
Error: error committing container for step {Env:[A_POSTGR_PORT=5435 A_POSTGR_USR=tu_lxoffic DISTTAG=f34container FGC=f34 container=oci EXT_LOGDIR=/var/log ADMIN_PWD=1geheim APACHE_LOGDIR=/var/log/httpd POSTGR_USR=tu_lxoffic POSTGR_PWD=1geheim POSTGR_DB=postgres POSTGR_HOST=kividb POSTGR_PORT=5435 POSTGR_DATADIR=/var/lib/pgsql/data POSTGR_LOGDIR=/var/lib/pgsql/data/log POSTGR_KIVI_AUTH_USR=postgres POSTGR_KIVI_AUTH_PWD=1geheim POSTGR_KIVI_AUTH_DB=kivitendo_auth POSTGR_KIVITST_USR=grtst POSTGR_KIVITST_PWD=1geheim TERM=vt100 locale=de_DE postgrespassword=1geheim KIVITENDO_VER=release-3.5.6.1 KIVITENDO_DATA=/var/www/kivitendoData PG_HOSTNAME= PG_DATA= APACHE_LOG_DIR=/var/log/httpd PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:run Args:[dnf -y install  `[[ ${POSTGR_HOST} != '127.0.0.1' &&  ${POSTGR_HOST} != 'localhost' ]] || echo postgresql-server`  httpd mod_fcgid  postgresql-contrib ...
texlive-scheme-medium texlive-collection-latex texlive-collection-latexextra   texlive-collection-latexrecommended texlive-collection-langgerman   texlive-collection-langenglish  langpacks-de git procps-ng}: error copying layers and metadata for container "047b8afeb1ded13044a1211e102ab6cee6c085c7fe874ace54074d5bb65e1361": Error initializing source containers-storage:834466bb4d44576dcac73d6a250772941daa44f62b3578ebb5c2b2a48cb86435-working-container: error extracting layer "b52227b3d4f0761ee82e8d8b1ee6322f521c627d1addd5c92f43d949d4808851": lgetxattr /mnt/raider/home2/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/b52227b3d4f0761ee82e8d8b1ee6322f521c627d1addd5c92f43d949d4808851/merged/var/cache/dnf/fedora-cisco-openh264-ffa960505abcfb54/repodata/3d50825fb25440abc9579f078205d6ba3590b1d146187af8179eb43214c8b40e-comps-Temporary.x86_64.xml.gz: software caused connection abort
`
Yes of course i checked via `ls -dZ` the KIVITENDO_DATA directory, that is located in the (tech-) users home directory.
Do you have any idea, why i received that message:
`error copying layers and metadata for container "537c264841188e82407cbe898f34d98071c0d718e895fc66bcb0057bfee2503d": Error initializing source containers-storage:511be9d72b4effbec63b6d8e0409d2d8817d35097df40d667181b2ef454e991a-working-container: error extracting layer "6fa0a74d49b34e6ff2163fe02e83aed0d48adb93cd173fb80b03093e0e4ea0c2": lgetxattr /mnt/raider/home2/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/6fa0a74d49b34e6ff2163fe02e83aed0d48adb93cd173fb80b03093e0e4ea0c2/merged/var/cache/dnf/fedora-cisco-openh264-ffa960505abcfb54/repodata/3d50825fb25440abc9579f078205d6ba3590b1d146187af8179eb43214c8b40e-comps-Temporary.x86_64.xml.gz: software caused connection abort
^C[1]+  Exit 125`

A connection abort sounds like a ressouce problem, am i right?


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. simply call  `podman build --tag kivirel:V0_6_0 --tmpdir=${DATA_STORE}/podmanTMP -v ${DATA_STORE}/data:/var/lib/pgsql/data:z  -v ${DATA_STORE}/logs:/var/log:z -v ${DATA_STORE}/rteFiles:/www/kivitendoData:z --build-arg  A_POSTGR_USR=tu_lxoffic --build-arg POSTGR_PWD=lx1geheim --build-arg   A_POSTGR_PORT=5435  --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs .`

2. 

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

It smells like a exhausted ressource somewhere in the code. 

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would exprect a docker image, that is generated on the F33 node.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Just a note, this tech-user account has been recreated.



**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman --version 
podman version 3.1.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: This could be we ran out of space on /var/tmp?

Salut Daniel

for this reason i set `--tmpdir=${DATA_STORE}/podmanTMP`! Well the storage resides on my raid, that should have enough room.

But i noticed, that there is a `/mnt/raider/home2/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/5f248be31ec11....40e-comps-Temporary.x86_64.xml.gz` file with a gz extension. Maybe the comprossing file operation is still using the tmp partition and does ignore my directive!

Update:

- Fedora F33 and F34 use tmpfs for their /tmp mount. I read, that the tmpfs filesystem may use swap space. So i added two real disk-partitions with 16GB each to the system, but the problem still remains
- Is there a workaround out  .. any ideas?

In order to cope suspected lack of disk space if moved the directory `/var/tmp` directory physically to a mounted raid device using a symbolic link. The new target directory shares the same selinux-attributes and has been _accepted_ by the os.


```
LANG=C  ls -lZ /mnt/raider/sys/vartmp
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 abrt abrt system_u:object_r:abrt_var_cache_t:s0  6 May 17 14:18 abrt
drwx------. 3 root root system_u:object_r:mnt_t:s0            25 May 24 11:19 systemd-private-b718ab0a384c432ea3d383a3a7ef93aa-ModemManager.service-ReD1FT
drwx------. 3 root root system_u:object_r:mnt_t:s0            25 May 24 11:19 systemd-private-b718ab0a384c432ea3d383a3a7ef93aa-chronyd.service-p1tJk5
drwx------. 3 root root system_u:object_r:mnt_t:s0            25 May 24 11:26 systemd-private-b718ab0a384c432ea3d383a3a7ef93aa-cockpit.service-6ouI2H
drwx------. 3 root root system_u:object_r:mnt_t:s0            25 May 24 11:19 systemd-private-b718ab0a384c432ea3d383a3a7ef93aa-dbus-broker.service-Ow7Rs0
drwx------. 3 root root system_u:object_r:mnt_t:s0            25 May 24 11:19 systemd-private-b718ab0a384c432ea3d383a3a7ef93aa-systemd-logind.service-5vaYhT
drwx------. 3 root root system_u:object_r:mnt_t:s0            17 May 24 11:19 systemd-private-b718ab0a384c432ea3d383a3a7ef93aa-systemd-oomd.service-zvnvbO
drwx------. 3 root root system_u:object_r:mnt_t:s0            17 May 24 11:19 systemd-private-b718ab0a384c432ea3d383a3a7ef93aa-systemd-resolved.service-clPxyR
drwx------. 3 root root system_u:object_r:mnt_t:s0            17 May 24 11:26 systemd-private-b718ab0a384c432ea3d383a3a7ef93aa-systemd-timedated.service-DoHj2q
```
So i ran the podman build again an was running into the same problem again, it is not the `/var/run ` directory, that caused the problem!

```
Complete!
Error: error committing container for step {Env:[A_POSTGR_USR=tu_lxoffic A_POSTGR_PORT=5435 DISTTAG=f34container FGC=f34 container=oci EXT_LOGDIR=/var/log ADMIN_PWD=1geheim APACHE_LOGDIR=/var/log/httpd ....
error copying layers and metadata for container "e0b67293053a84200efa3f576b0938615e5a5bf324e393e322d4ea0b477899cc": Error initializing source containers-storage:aa366b9d629f0d6e76287c9df89929c0b1c0c7c867d55c3ad5fdf9d56fe1d637-working-container: error extracting layer "a99b1b6251d33cf91ebf0968a79398885b0cbff161908f7bf7ff053f7fd7d907": lgetxattr /mnt/raider/home2/techlxoffice/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/a99b1b6251d33cf91ebf0968a79398885b0cbff161908f7bf7ff053f7fd7d907/merged/var/cache/dnf/fedora-cisco-openh264-ffa960505abcfb54/repodata/3d50825fb25440abc9579f078205d6ba3590b1d146187af8179eb43214c8b40e-comps-Temporary.x86_64.xml.gz: software caused connection abort
```

While running the `podman build` i checked the ressource consumtion:
- the entire system with 16Gb did not use any paging 
- there was no tmp var/tmp directory, that was running out of available space
- the only indication of a ressource bottleneck was the CPU consumption, that grew touser:88%, while the IO rate went down. 
- when i replace the `FROM fedora` by `FROM fedora:33` the podman-build, the build process is successful. In this case podman-build would load the base-image from Fedora-F33 and not the latest F34. 

I looks like the `podman build` was running itself or triggering another program, that was running a virtuel machine or a dynamic memory space with a limited capacity. Maybe a garbage collector was running wild?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@groovyman Do you still have this issue.  The TMPDIR setting should be used to redirect where podman stores temporary images pulled from the internet --tmpdir is just for storing container runtime content, not image content.

Salut Dan, (@rhatdan )

well as a workaround i exchanged the dockerfile from 
> FROM fedora
to
FROM fedora:33

and i got my image. Well, after a couple of updates, let's give him a change. ...

Salut @rhatdan

I was suggesting something like this^^

Salut Daniel,
i already tried  this, but with no success. Besides i also executed a export directive.
-- first run 
`
$ TMPDIR=${DATA_STORE}/podmanTMP/tmp LANG=C podman build --tag kivirel:V0_6_1t00_34 --tmpdir=${DATA_STORE}/podmanTMP -v ${DATA_STORE}/data:/var/lib/pgsql/data:z -v ${DATA_STORE}/logs:/var/log:z -v ${DATA_STORE}/rteFiles:/www/kivitendoData:z --build-arg A_POSTGR_USR=tu_lxoffic --build-arg POSTGR_PWD=lx1geheim --build-arg A_POSTGR_PORT=5435 --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs .
`
-- next try
`
$ export TMPDIR=${DATA_STORE}/podmanTMP/tmp
$ export LANG=C
$  podman build --tag kivirel:V0_6_1t00_34 --tmpdir=${DATA_STORE}/podmanTMP -v ${DATA_STORE}/data:/var/lib/pgsql/data:z -v ${DATA_STORE}/logs:/var/log:z -v ${DATA_STORE}/rteFiles:/www/kivitendoData:z --build-arg A_POSTGR_USR=tu_lxoffic --build-arg POSTGR_PWD=lx1geheim --build-arg A_POSTGR_PORT=5435 --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs .
`
